Package neqsim.process.diagnostics

Class FailurePropagationTracer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.diagnostics.FailurePropagationTracer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class FailurePropagationTracer extends Object implements Serializable
Traces how a failure propagates through a process flowsheet.

Given an initial trip event on a piece of equipment, this class determines the sequence of downstream equipment that will be affected, estimates the propagation timing, and builds a complete failure cascade chain. It answers the question: "How did the failure propagate through the plant?"

The tracer uses the ProcessTopologyAnalyzer for flowsheet connectivity and the DependencyAnalyzer for cascade impact analysis. It extends the static dependency analysis with time-ordered propagation and severity assessment.

Usage example: 

 FailurePropagationTracer tracer = new FailurePropagationTracer(processSystem);
 PropagationResult result = tracer.trace("Compressor-1");

 for (PropagationStep step : result.getSteps()) {
   System.out.printf("%.0fs: %s (%s)%n", step.getEstimatedDelaySeconds(),
       step.getEquipmentName(), step.getEffect());
 }
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:
  • TripEventDetector
  • RootCauseAnalyzer
  • DependencyAnalyzer

  • Constructor Details

    • FailurePropagationTracer

      public FailurePropagationTracer(ProcessSystem processSystem)
      Creates a failure propagation tracer.
      Parameters:
      processSystem - the process system to trace through
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if processSystem is null

  • Method Details

    • initializeAnalyzers

      private void initializeAnalyzers()
      Initializes the topology and dependency analyzers.

    • setCustomDelay

      public void setCustomDelay(String sourceEquipment, String targetEquipment, double delaySeconds)
      Sets a custom propagation delay between two equipment items.
      Parameters:
      sourceEquipment - source equipment name
      targetEquipment - target equipment name
      delaySeconds - propagation delay in seconds

    • setMaxCascadeDepth

      public void setMaxCascadeDepth(int depth)
      Sets the maximum cascade depth.
      Parameters:
      depth - maximum depth (default 20)

    • trace

      public FailurePropagationTracer.PropagationResult trace(String failedEquipmentName)
      Traces the failure propagation from a given equipment.
      Parameters:
      failedEquipmentName - name of the initially failed equipment
      Returns:
      propagation result with ordered steps

    • trace

      public FailurePropagationTracer.PropagationResult trace(TripEvent tripEvent)
      Traces propagation from a trip event.
      Parameters:
      tripEvent - the trip event that initiated the failure
      Returns:
      propagation result

    • getDelay

      private double getDelay(String source, String target, boolean direct)
      Gets the propagation delay between two equipment items.
      Parameters:
      source - source equipment name
      target - target equipment name
      direct - true if directly connected
      Returns:
      delay in seconds

    • classifyEffect

      private String classifyEffect(String source, String target)
      Classifies the effect of a failure propagation on a downstream equipment.
      Parameters:
      source - failed equipment name
      target - affected equipment name
      Returns:
      description of the effect

    • assessImpact

      private FailurePropagationTracer.PropagationStep.ImpactLevel assessImpact(String equipmentName)
      Assesses the impact level on a given equipment.
      Parameters:
      equipmentName - equipment name
      Returns:
      impact level

      name - equipment name
      Returns:
